posts.text:
📢 Looking for Software Developer Interview (University Project)

Hi everyone 👋

I’m currently working on a university group project for the subject CSD34203 Special Topics in Software Development, focusing on software development technologies and real-world industry challenges.

We are looking for a Software Developer / IT professional who could spare some time for a short interview (online preferred).

Topics include:

* Company background & developer role
* Technologies used in real-world software development (AI, Cloud Computing, DevOps, etc.)
* Challenges faced in software engineering practices

This interview is strictly for academic purposes only and will be used as part of our project submission.

If you’re interested or know someone who might be able to help, feel free to DM me or contact me via email:

📧 safwanaman2003@gmail.com

Really appreciate any support 🙌

Thank you in advance!

posts.text:
Alhamdulillah, I have successfully completed the CyberSAFE Untuk Rakyat course by Rakyat Digital.

Through this course, I learned more about cybersecurity awareness, online safety, and the importance of protecting personal information while using digital platforms. It was a really good experience and gave me better exposure to how important cybersecurity is in today’s digital world.

There’s still a lot for me to learn, but I’m grateful to keep improving my knowledge little by little.

Special thanks to Danish Azam for sharing his LinkedIn post about this course earlier. Saw his post and decided to give it a try too 👍

If you’re interested, you can try the course here:
https://lnkd.in/gUiNzy7w

Bismillah and Goodluck!! 😊

#CyberSAFE #CyberSecurity #RakyatDigital #DigitalLearning #CyberAwareness

posts.text:
Still can't believe it's already been 2 years since I completed the Mandarin Language Course HSK Level 1 class at UniSZA. Time really flies.

One of the most challenging but fun subjects I've taken and definitely pushed me out of my comfort zone to learn a third language.

Big thanks to Laoshi Afiqah for making the class enjoyable and memorable.

#Throwback #Mandarin #HSK1 #UniSZA

posts.text:
Grateful for the opportunity to join the Road To Excellence (RTE) 2026 program organised for JPA sponsored students at UniSZA together with my friend, Ahmad Ikram Kamalludin . 

The program provided valuable exposure on career pathways, post-study contract obligations, and preparation for the working world through insightful sharing sessions from JPA officers, SPA, SPP, and alumni panels.  

Thank you to all speakers and organisers for the meaningful experience and guidance. Looking forward to applying the knowledge gained for my future career journey. ✨  

#RTE2026 #JPA #UniSZA #CareerDevelopment #JPAScholar

posts.text:
I’m really grateful to have had a 1-to-1 coaching session with Puan Aisyah Nurul Atsirah Aisyah Azman , a career advisor.

We started with a mock interview, and honestly I learned a lot from it. The feedback she gave was very helpful for me to understand how I can improve and present myself better in future interviews.

After that, she also shared some practical tips on how to build my LinkedIn presence, how to position myself professionally, and how to connect and grow my network in a more meaningful way.

We also had a short sharing session where we got to know each other better. It turned into a very genuine and meaningful conversation, and I really appreciate the advice and encouragement shared throughout the session.

Overall, I’m truly thankful for the time and guidance. It was a valuable experience for me and definitely something I will carry forward.
posts.imageAlt: I’m really grateful to have had a 1-to-1 coaching session with Puan Aisyah Nurul Atsirah Aisyah Azman , a career advisor

posts.text:
I had the opportunity to join an insightful webinar recently on resume building, internship readiness, and career positioning, and I genuinely walked away with a completely upgraded mindset.

A big appreciation to Nurul Atsirah Aisyah Azman for delivering such a practical and eye-opening session. It wasn’t just a theory, but it felt like a real-world guide on how to transition from student thinking to industry-ready execution.

Some key takeaways that really stood out for me:

• Your resume is not a list, it’s a strategy
 It’s about positioning yourself for both ATS systems and human recruiters by clearly showing impact, not just tasks.

• Internships should be treated like real roles
 Go beyond assigned work and take ownership, ask for feedback, and actively seek learning opportunities.

• Employability is built, not given
 Exposure + Skills + Networking + Evidence of work = stronger career positioning.

• Documentation matters
 What you achieve is only valuable if you can clearly communicate and present it.

This session really helped me realign my approach to my upcoming internship journey and personal development in tech. A special thank you to Fahim Najme Ikhwan for encouraging me to join this session in the first place. I really appreciate the push. It turned out to be a meaningful and productive learning experience.

Grateful for the knowledge shared and definitely looking forward to applying everything learned moving forward.

#CareerDevelopment #Internship #SoftwareDevelopment #ProfessionalGrowth #ResumeBuilding #Networking #StudentToProfessional
posts.imageAlt: I had the opportunity to join an insightful webinar recently on resume building, internship readiness, and career positi

posts.text:
Done task for SEEK Asia, and some insights extracted from JobStreet (Nov 2014 – Jan 2015):

Overall insight:

1. Adoption rate is low, around 25%
Only about 25% of people who hire actually use the features. Maybe the features aren't interesting enough or people don't fully understand them.

2. CV access is very high
This is proof that hirers are basically picking their candidates. Basically, quality > quantity.

3. Message usage is low
Most of the people who hire on JobStreet don't really use messaging because the credit is quite expensive.

4. There are power users
A small group of hirers use JobStreet effectively, but most users don't fully utilise the platform.

5. High sessions but low actions
People who want to hire are interested in JobStreet, but that doesn't mean they will do anything. The platform doesn't fully deliver value.
